mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% and remains there for at least one month, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding, signaling exhausted monetary accommodation. This regime shift causes equity risk premiums to widen as investors recognize limited policy support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. This employment contraction reduces consumer income expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking markets price in demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k persons in any single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months following PAYEMS declines > 100k, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move together as leading indicators of aggregate demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this joint signal indicates demand destruction across consumer and residential investment channels. The interaction of these two demand shocks produces amplified negative equity returns over 20 trading days due to multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% AND HOUST declines > 2% in same month
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST declines do not predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression against 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth below 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization constraints. In a low-inflation environment (CPI growth < 2% annually), this supply weakness without offsetting demand pressure creates stagflationary risk. Equity markets reprice downward over 60 trading days as investors recognize earnings margin compression from rising input costs without pricing power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO weakness with low CPI are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL does not improve predictive power over single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) serves as an always-on inverse predictor of equity returns. Higher unemployment reflects labor market slack, reduced consumer spending capacity, and lower corporate earnings. A 1 percentage point increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through the demand destruction channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter serves as an always-on trend signal for equity returns. Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized indicate robust demand and earnings growth, supporting positive equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously across all regimes as a fundamental growth signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% do not produce positive average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime (UNRATE < 5% and GDPC1 growth > 2%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary policy tightening bias. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative 20-day equity returns as markets anticipate further rate hikes and earnings margin compression from higher financing costs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3% AND UNRATE < 5%
failure_condition: The three-way interaction of FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL, and UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.06, or the mechanism fails to activate in late-cycle periods (UNRATE < 5% and GDPC1 > 2%) when inflation and rates are elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 6%, signaling a post-shock recovery phase where supply-side improvements outpace labor market healing. This asymmetric recovery pattern produces positive 5-day equity returns as markets recognize productivity gains and earnings upside before full employment is restored.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 1.5% and UNRATE > 6% do not produce positive average 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in explaining 5-day forward returns.
